title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging
label: mx1_review_20
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: The transformation is valid if and only if for every node in the initial tree, its children can be partitioned into contiguous segments such that each segment corresponds to exactly one child of the corresponding node in the target tree, and the subtree structure rooted at the segment's representative in the initial tree matches the subtree structure of the child in the target tree.
final_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: The transformation is valid if and only if for every node in the initial tree, its children can be partitioned into contiguous segments such that each segment corresponds to exactly one child of the corresponding node in the target tree, and the subtree structure rooted at the segment's representative in the initial tree matches the subtree structure of the child in the target tree.

root_orchestration.decision_notes: The child result provides the core logic for the transformation condition. I will format this into the final required response.

grading.grading_notes: The problem is a coding task (USACO Gold level) requiring the implementation of an algorithm to reconstruct a sequence of operations. The authoritative reference indicates that grading requires 'binary_hidden_tests' (execution of code against hidden test cases). The submitted answer is a text-based explanation/justification rather than a code solution. Since I cannot execute code to verify if a solution passes the hidden tests, and the benchmark lacks a text-based ground truth for a conceptual answer, I must set the status to ungraded.
